sensitivity.description: Sensitivity of the polygraph (lie-detector), i.e., the probability of correctly detecting a liar. The answer uses 1 - sensitivity = 0.24 as the probability that a lying drug user passes the test.

specificity.description: Specificity of the polygraph, i.e., the probability that a non-drug user (truth-teller) passes the test. Used directly in the denominator and numerator of the final proportion calculation.

drug_user_prevalence.description: Proportion of potential donors who use drugs (5%). Used in the calculation of the proportion of blood donations from drug users.

proportion_lying.description: Proportion of drug users who lie about their drug use (one third ≈ 0.33). Two thirds admit and are excluded; the remaining third undergo polygraph testing.
